title: ophrys aranifera subsp. praecox
intro: Ophrys aranifera subsp. praecox, also known as early spider orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as relieving menstrual cramps, soothing digestive issues, and reducing fever. The early spider orchid is typically prepared in herbal teas or infusions, as well as in tinctures or dried forms, for medicinal use.
